properties.name: Community Information
properties.location: between the townships of  Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand
properties.webBody:
This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand. 
  • This Community Information replaces the message issued on 17 February 2026. 
  • There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
  • Smoke may be visible from nearby communities and roads. 
  • Firefighters are currently patrolling this fire.  
  • Please observe all track closures in National Park and State Forest.    
There is currently no threat to communities however be aware of crews and heavy machinery working to rehabilitate affected tracks and stabilising areas of the fire ground.  
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.  
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
properties.text:
COMMUNITY INFORMATION - BUSHFIRE - Stay Informed
Incident Location: Carlisle River
Issue Date: Tuesday 24 February 2026, 10:36 am
Next Update: Tuesday 03 March 2026, 12:40 pm

More details at http://emergency.vic.gov.au/respond/#!/warning/41496/moreinfo

This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of  Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand. 
- This Community Information replaces the message issued on 17 February 2026. 
- There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
- Smoke may be visible from nearby communities and roads. 
- Firefighters are currently patrolling this fire.  
- Please observe all track closures in National Park and State Forest.    


There is currently no threat to communities however be aware of crews and heavy machinery working to rehabilitate affected tracks and stabilising areas of the fire ground.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: the area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement
properties.webBody:
This Advice message is being issued for the area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement.
  • There is a bushfire at Mansfield-Woods Point Rd, Gaffneys Creek that is not yet under control.
  • This Advice replaces the Advice- Threat is Reduced issued at 7.05pm Tuesday 24th February 2026.
  • There is currently no threat to communities, but you should continue to stay informed and monitor conditions.
  • Be aware that in fire affected areas, rainfall runoff into waterways may contain debris such as ash, soil, trees, and rocks. Heavy rainfall may also increase the potential for landslides and debris across roads. 
  • Monitor weather warnings, forecasts, and river levels through the VicEmergency website/app/hotline.
What you should do: 
  • Monitor weather conditions and warnings. 
  • Review your fire survival plan. 
  • If you do not have a plan, decide what you will do if the situation changes.
Impacts in your area: 
  • The following roads are closed: Mansfield - Woods point Road is closed from Knockwood to Frenchmans Gap.
  • Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
  • Firefighters are responding to this fire.
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

The next update is expected by 02/03/2026 11:30 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
  • Community Newsletter - Click here
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service on 1800 555 677 and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Glenelg and Hopkins Rivers and Portland Coast
properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for Glenelg and Hopkins Rivers and Portland Coast.
  • Minor Flooding and areas of localised flooding possible across the flood watch area from overnight Sunday into Monday.
  • A slow moving low pressure trough is forecast to approach the western border during Sunday, with areas of widespread moderate to heavy rainfall and embedded thunderstorms developing over the west, particularly near the border.
  • The trough is expected to slowly cross western and central Victoria during Monday, with possible moderate to locally heavy falls.
  • Heavy rainfall totals are expected around parts of the north west and south west catchments of Victoria from Sunday.
  • Accumulated rainfall totals may be significant.
  • Catchments are relatively dry across the Flood Watch area, with some areas slightly wetter due to rainfall observed since last Sunday morning. River and creek level rises, and areas of minor flooding may develop from overnight Sunday into Monday.
  • Localised flooding and overland inundation is also possible within the flood watch area.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/emergencies/flood.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. 
  • Review your emergency plan and check your emergency kit is fully stocked, if you have one.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
  • For more information on road closures go to https://traffic.transport.vic.gov.au/. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 28/02/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

More details at http://emergency.vic.gov.au/respond/#!/warning/41585/moreinfo

This Flood Watch is being issued for Glenelg and Hopkins Rivers and Portland Coast.

- A slow moving low pressure trough is forecast to approach the western border during Sunday, with areas of widespread moderate to heavy rainfall and embedded thunderstorms developing over the west, particularly near the border. 
- The trough is expected to slowly cross western and central Victoria during Monday, with possible moderate to locally heavy falls. 
- Heavy rainfall totals are expected around parts of the north west and south west catchments of Victoria from Sunday. 
- Accumulated rainfall totals may be significant. 
- Catchments are relatively dry across the Flood Watch area, with some areas slightly wetter due to rainfall observed since last Sunday morning. River and creek level rises, and areas of minor flooding may develop from overnight Sunday into Monday. 
- Localised flooding and overland inundation is also possible within the flood watch area. 



Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for Avoca River.

  • A slow moving low pressure trough is forecast to approach the western border during Sunday, with areas of widespread moderate to heavy rainfall and embedded thunderstorms developing over the west, particularly near the border.
  • The trough is expected to slowly cross western and central Victoria during Monday, with possible moderate to locally heavy falls.
  • Heavy rainfall totals are expected around parts of the north west and south west catchments of Victoria from Sunday. Accumulated rainfall totals may be significant.
  • Catchments are relatively dry across the Flood Watch area, with some areas slightly wetter due to rainfall observed since last Sunday morning.
  • River and creek level rises, and areas of minor flooding may develop from overnight Sunday into Monday.
  • Localised flooding and overland inundation is also possible within the Flood Watch area.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads.
  • Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous.
  • Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 28/02/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:

  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
